task_content: Add sqlite-vec dependency and register it on startup for the bundled SQLite connection.
detection_standard: Startup must fail clearly if sqlite-vec is unavailable; vec_version() self-check must pass before the worker starts.

task_content: Extend the SQLite executor with typed vector jobs while preserving serialized execution and existing execute_sql behavior.
detection_standard: execute_sql tests must still pass; vector jobs must use the same worker thread, timeout handling, and readonly config.

task_content: Add vector request and response structs for collection creation, upsert, search, delete, and drop responses.
detection_standard: Serde schema generation must expose required fields and JSON responses must include success, optional error, and elapsed_ms.

task_content: Implement collection name, dimension, vector, metadata, filter, id, and top_k validation.
detection_standard: Invalid names, __ prefix, zero dimensions, empty ids, dimension mismatch, non-finite values, non-object metadata, non-object filter, unsupported filter values, and top_k greater than max_top_k must return stable JSON errors.
Registry table must be created on demand; created_at must be UTC ISO-8601 text; registry rows must match table names, dimension, and cosine metric.

task_content: Implement create_vector_collection with idempotent same-dimension behavior.
detection_standard: First call must create vec_<collection>; repeated same-dimension call must return created=false; repeated different-dimension call must fail without changing the existing collection.

task_content: Implement covering upsert_vectors for batch vector writes.
detection_standard: Batch must be atomic; existing id must replace vector, text, and metadata; missing text becomes NULL; missing metadata becomes {}; any invalid item must roll back the whole batch.

task_content: Implement unfiltered search_vectors using sqlite-vec KNN query.
detection_standard: Search must order by cosine distance, respect top_k, return id, distance, text, metadata, and never return the stored vector field.

task_content: Implement simple JSON metadata equality filtering for search_vectors.
detection_standard: Filter must match only top-level simple keys with scalar values; comparisons must be type-sensitive; filtered results must be correctly ranked by cosine distance; performance is not required to match unfiltered KNN.

task_content: Implement delete_vectors for batch id deletion.
detection_standard: Deleting existing and missing ids must succeed; response must report requested_count and deleted_count; operation must reject in readonly mode.

task_content: Implement drop_vector_collection and expose it in tools/list.
detection_standard: Drop must remove the vec0 table and registry row; missing collection must return success with existed=false; operation must reject in readonly mode.

task_content: Enforce readonly mode across all vector tools.
detection_standard: search_vectors must work in readonly mode; create, upsert, delete, and drop must return readonly errors without modifying the database.

task_content: Wire all five vector tools into the MCP server.
detection_standard: tools/list must return execute_sql plus create_vector_collection, upsert_vectors, search_vectors, delete_vectors, and drop_vector_collection; tools/call must return JSON text for each vector tool.

task_content: Verify direct execute_sql compatibility with vector collection tables.
detection_standard: After creating and upserting through vector tools, execute_sql must be able to inspect __vector_collections and query vec_<collection> rows directly.

task_content: Document vector setup, tools, examples, readonly behavior, and filtered-search performance limits.
detection_standard: README must show create, upsert, search, delete, drop, execute_sql inspection examples, and note that filtered search is correct but not KNN-optimized in the first version.

task_content: Run full formatting, lint-equivalent checks, and tests after implementation.
detection_standard: The final implementation must be formatted, compile cleanly, and pass the complete test suite.
